DELAYED REPORT: DIME SIZE HAIL FELL AND COVERED THE GROUND. A TREE WAS BLOWN OVER ALONG 28 MILE ROAD, WHICH DOWNED SOME POWERLINES. POWER OUTAGES LASTED APPROXIMATELY 8 HOURS.

NUMEROUS HEALTHY POPLAR TREES OF AROUND A FOOT AND A HALF IN DIAMETER WERE BLOCKING PORTIONS OF U.S. 127 NEAR SNOW SNAKE SKI AND GOLF. TREES WERE DOWN ON THE GOLF COURSE AS WELL. TIME IS ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
SMALL BRANCHES DOWN AND A TABLE FLIPPED OVER. RELAYED BY WOOD TV. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
TWO REPORTS OF POWER LINES DOWN. ALSO GETTING REPORTS OF POWER OUTAGES IN FREMONT AS WELL.

DELAYED REPORT: STATE PARK EMPLOYEE REPORTED THAT SEVERAL TREES ARE DOWN THROUGHOUT THE PARK. STORM CLEAN-UP IS UNDERWAY. EMPLOYEE ALSO NOTED TREES DOWN AT THEIR OWN RESIDENCE, 2 MILES EAST OF THE PARK.
MEASURED ON BOAT ANEMOMETER AT GREAT LAKES MARINA .
MANY 6-12 INCH DIAMETER TREES DOWN ALONGSIDE SHETTLER RD EAST OF US-31. .
9 INCH TREES DOWN AND LOCALIZED WATER OVER THE ROAD AT W SHERMAN BLVD AND 9TH STREET.
BUOY STATION PORT SHELDON BUOY /45029/.
TWO BRANCHES DOWN. BIGGEST IS 7-8 INCHES IN DIAMETER OFF A HEALTHY TREE.

COUNTY DISPATCH REPORTED SEVERAL TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN VAN BUREN COUNTY. IT IS UNCERTAIN WHAT ROLE LIGHTNING PLAYED IN ADDITION TO WIND IN CAUSING THE DAMAGE.

MULTIPLE TREES REPORTED DOWN IN SHERIDAN TWP EAST OF CLARE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED ON PRIVATE PROPERTY JUST OFF M-21. RESIDENT REPORTED THAT A BRANCH IMPALED A GARAGE WALL. RESIDENT ALSO STATED THAT THERE IS TREE DAMAGE AT BERTHA BROCK PARK. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
TREES REPORTED DOWN OVER M-21 JUST WEST OF THE PRISON NEAR IONIA.
POWER LINES REPORTED DOWN IN IONIA

HEALTHY PINE TREE 4 FEET IN DIAMETER SNAPPED ABOUT 12 FEET OFF THE GROUND DOING DAMAGE TO POWER LINES. ONE HOME WAS REPORTED AS BEING EVACUATED. DELTA TWP FIRE IS ON THE SCENE. TIME OF OCCURENCE EST BY RADAR.
EM REPORTS SCATTERED AREAS WITH POWER LINES AND TREE BRANCHES DOWN ACROSS THE SOUTHERN END OF THE COUNTY. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

-DELAYED REPORT-PUBLIC CALLER REPORTED A 1 FOOT DIAMETER TREE LIMB DOWN AT THEIR RESIDENCE. SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWN NEAR M-43 ALONG THE EATON/INGHAM COUNTY LINE. CALLER DID NOTE THAT SOME OF THE TREES SHOWED SIGNS OF ROT. WIND ESTIMATED AROUND 65 MPH. TIME OF OCCURENCE ESTIMATE BY RADAR.
TREE BLOWN OVER ONTO POWER LINES NEAR WRIGHT ROAD AND CLARK ROAD IN EAGLE. PUBLIC REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.

SPOTTER NOTED DAMAGE TO AN AWNING ON HIS NEIGHBORS HOME. A UTILITY SHED WAS BLOWN FROM THE YARD INTO ANOTHER NEIGHBORS YARD. A NINE INCH TREE LIMB WAS BROKEN FROM A TREE AND POWER LINES WERE DOWN IN THE AREA.
PICTURES SENT BY THE EM. LARGE TREE DOWN ACROSS THE ROAD NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF OKEMOS ROAD AND RABY ROAD.
HALF INCH HAIL AND WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED NEAR 60 MPH
EM RELAYED REPORT FROM THE PUBLIC
MAX THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST RECORDED AT LANSING CAPITAL AIRPORT.
APPROXIMATELY THE TOP 10 FEET OF A 30 FEET PINE WAS SNAPPED OFF. WINDS WERE ESTIMATE AROUND 50 MPH.
TREE APPROXIMATELY 10 INCHES IN DIAMETER REPORTED DOWN. EMERGENCY MANAGER RELAYED THIS REPORT FROM A TRAINED STORM SPOTTER.

REPORTED AT 1950 32 STREET SE, GRAND RAPIDS A 40 FOOT TALL TREE 14 INCHES DIAMETER HEALTHY WAS SNAPPED 4 INCHES ABOVE THE GROUND. NO DAMAGE TO NEAR BY STRUCTURES WAS REPORTED. THERE WERE NUMEROUS POWER OUTAGES REPORTED NORTH OF 28TH STREET.
HEALTHY 18 INCH DIAMETER TREE DOWN. REPORTED AT 68 ST AND DIVISION AVE.

TWO TO THREE INCH DIAMETER TREE LIMBS DOWN. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BRIEFLY NEAR RIVERSIDE ROAD. ONE OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED. TWO HOMES HAD MINOR SIDING OR ROOFING DAMAGE FROM DEBRIS AND FALLEN TREES. SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH THAT WAS ABOUT ONE QUARTER MILE LONG AND 25-50 YARDS WIDE.
AN OUTBUILDING WAS DEMOLISHED ON RIVERSIDE ROAD. A TREE FELL ON A NEARBY HOUSE, RESULTING IN ROOF DAMAGE. REPORT RELAYED BY WOOD TV.

911 CALL CENTER RELAYED A CALL FROM THE PUBLIC. EXTENSIVE WIND DAMAGE BEING OBSERVED IN WYOMING. SEVERAL TREES DOWN IN THE AREA. POSSIBLE DAMAGE TO HOUSE. EXTENT OF DAMAGE TO HOUSE IS UNKNOWN. .

DEBRIS IN THE AIR NEAR 40TH AND IVANREST.
TREES DOWN ON HOUSES NEAR 32ND ST AND HIGHGATE AVE ALSO 2900 BLK LONGSTREET AVE. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

A BRIEF EF-O TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN KENT COUNTY NEAR PERKINS AVENUE NE BETWEEN LEONARD STREET NE AND KNAPP STREET NE. TREE DAMAGE AND SOME PROPERTY DAMAGE FROM FALLEN TREES OCCURRED AS THE RESULT OF THIS BRIEF TORNADO.
RECEIVED A CALL FROM THE PUBLIC REPORTING SEVERAL DOWNED TREE LIMBS. LARGEST LIMB DOWNED IN HER YARD IS 6 INCHES IN DIAMETER. .

NWS DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO IN IONIA AND MONTCALM COUNTIES. TORNADO DAMAGE BEGAN JUST EAST OF THE SMALL TOWN OF ORLEANS AND MOVED NORTHEAST WHERE IT CROSSED M-44... BRINGING DOWN SEVERAL LARGE TREES. ONE OF THE TREES FELL ON A HOUSE. THE TORNADO THEN CROSSED WEST LONG LAKE ROAD WHERE THE CONCRETE BLOCK WALL OF A GARAGE WAS BLOWN OUT AND SHINGLES WERE PEELED OFF THE ROOF OF A HOUSE. PEAK WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 8 MILES WITH A WIDTH OF AROUND 100 YARDS.

RADAR INDICATED TORNADO. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN...WITH ONE ACROSS ROAD. LIMBS ON POWER LINES. SEVERAL PINE TREES SNAPPED OFF 5 TO 6 FEET OFF OF GROUND. POWER OUT. TIME OF REPORT ESTIMATED FROM RADAR DATA.
